chunks.text: Fashion week, Paris.
The world's top designers present their new collections.
The models walking the runway look like beautiful paintings.
They wear high heel shoes and gorgeous dresses.
A crown of flowers decorates their heads, as if they were queens.
Backstage, staff run about in a hurry.
" Shit, we have no time," someone mutters.
Makeup corrections, hair adjustments, final checks on costumes.
The smell of staff meals drifts from the pan.
After long hours of work, both staff and models are hungry.
The designer apologizes, " I am sorry we beg you to work without even water.
But once the show begins, everything is beautiful like magic.
With each step the models take, the venue holds its breath.
Fashion is not mere clothing.
It is a means of self - expression and a statement to society.
In recent years, the fashion industry has been forced to change greatly.
Environmental issues, workers' rights, diversity.
Criticism of fast fashion is rising.
Young designers advocate ethical fashion.
Use of recycled materials, fair labor conditions, accommodation of diverse body types.
" Clothes should be made in a way that does not sacrifice the earth.
Major brands too are slowly changing.
The pressure of " if we do not change
we will not survive " moves the whole industry.
Fashion is a mirror of its era.
The clothes we choose reveal what we value.
